markdown:
<title
    level="1"
    right="Feat 8"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Shamble](/Feats.aspx?ID=3564) <actions string="Two Actions or Three Actions" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Archetype" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=12" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Book of the Dead](/Sources.aspx?ID=118) pg. 61

**Archetype** [Zombie](/Archetypes.aspx?ID=182)

**Prerequisites**
[Zombie Dedication](/Feats.aspx?ID=3557)

</column>

---

You may be slower than you were in life, but your pace is unfaltering. Stride twice, or three times if you spent 3 actions to Shamble. During this movement, you ignore difficult terrain and greater difficult terrain.

 You can choose to ignore all circumstance and status penalties to your Speed when you Shamble. If you do, you must succeed at a DC 6 flat check when you finish Shambling or your body deteriorates.
name: Shamble

markdown:
<title
    level="1"
    right="Feat 8"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Unkillable](/Feats.aspx?ID=3565) <actions string="Reaction" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Archetype" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=12" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Book of the Dead](/Sources.aspx?ID=118) pg. 61

**Archetype** [Zombie](/Archetypes.aspx?ID=182)

**Frequency**
once per hour

**Prerequisites**
[Zombie Dedication](/Feats.aspx?ID=3557)

**Trigger**
You would be reduced to 0 HP.

</column>

---

You're relentless and nigh unkillable, shrugging off blows that should destroy you, though they might make your body start to fall apart. You are reduced to 1 HP instead of 0 HP. You must succeed at a DC 6 flat check or your body deteriorates.
name: Unkillable

<title
    level="1"
    right="Feat 12"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Festering Wound](/Feats.aspx?ID=3566) <actions string="" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Archetype" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=12" />
<trait label="Disease" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=46" />
<trait label="Divine" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=48" />
<trait label="Necromancy" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=117" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Book of the Dead](/Sources.aspx?ID=118) pg. 61

**Archetype** [Zombie](/Archetypes.aspx?ID=182)

**Prerequisites**
[Zombie Dedication](/Feats.aspx?ID=3557)

</column>

---

Wounds you inflict with your [unarmed](/Traits.aspx?ID=199) attacks fester and rot, becoming infected and difficult to heal. The DC to [Treat these Wounds](/Actions.aspx?ID=57) using [Medicine](/Skills.aspx?ID=9) increases by 5, and magically or alchemically healing these wounds requires a [counteract](/Rules.aspx?ID=371) check against your class DC or spell DC, whichever is higher. Succeeding at any of these removes the disease. The effect also ends if the creature is healed to full Hit Points by any means.
name: Festering Wound

<title
    level="1"
    right="Feat 14"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Seize](/Feats.aspx?ID=3567) <actions string="Single Action" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Archetype" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=12" />
<trait label="Flourish" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=73" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Book of the Dead](/Sources.aspx?ID=118) pg. 61

**Archetype** [Zombie](/Archetypes.aspx?ID=182)

**Prerequisites**
[Zombie Dedication](/Feats.aspx?ID=3557)

**Requirements**
Your last action was a successful fist Strike against a creature no more than one size larger than you.

</column>

---

You seize your foe and pull it close. The foe you hit is [grabbed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=20) as if you succeeded at an [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) check to [Grapple](/Actions.aspx?ID=35) the foe.
name: Seize
requirement_markdown: Your last action was a successful fist Strike against a creature no more than one size larger than you.

markdown:
<title
    level="1"
    right="Feat 2"
    pfs="Limited"
>
[Alkenstar Agent Dedication](/Feats.aspx?ID=3568) <actions string="" />
</title>

<spoilers>May contain spoilers from Outlaws of Alkenstar</spoilers>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Archetype" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=12" />
<trait label="Dedication" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=41" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Pathfinder #178: Punks in a Powderkeg](/Sources.aspx?ID=119) pg. 78

**Archetype** [Alkenstar Agent](/Archetypes.aspx?ID=183)

**Prerequisites**
Trained in [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=5); trained at least one [firearm](/WeaponGroups.aspx?ID=16)

**Access**
You have been initiated by the grand duchess of Alkenstar or one of her agents.

</column>

---

You put on the persona of a rough and tumble outlaw, but your real goals are to report threats to Alkenstar back to the grand duchess. You become an expert in Deception and trained in your choice of Underworld [Lore](/Skills.aspx?ID=8) or Legal Lore; if you were already trained, you become an expert instead. You gain the [Lie to Me](/Feats.aspx?ID=811) skill feat.

**Special** You can't select another dedication feat until you've gained two other feats from the Alkenstar Agent archetype.

<title level="2">Alkenstar Agent Dedication leads to...</title>
[Flash your Badge](/Feats.aspx?ID=3571), [Loose Cannon](/Feats.aspx?ID=3569), [Prepare Papers](/Feats.aspx?ID=3570)
name: Alkenstar Agent Dedication
prerequisite_markdown: Trained in [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=5); trained at least one [firearm](/WeaponGroups.aspx?ID=16)

markdown:
**Source** [Pathfinder #178: Punks in a Powderkeg](/Sources.aspx?ID=119) pg. 78 **Archetype** [Alkenstar Agent](/Archetypes.aspx?ID=183) **Prerequisites** [Alkenstar Agent Dedication](/Feats.aspx?ID=3568) --- Your unpredictable gunfire often catches enemies off guard or hits unprotected areas. Make a ranged Strike with a firearm against a creature that you haven't already attacked on this turn. You gain a circumstance bonus to damage on this Strike equal to the number of weapon damage dice. The Strike gains the following failure effect. **Failure** The firearm [misfires](/Rules.aspx?ID=1649), but it doesn't cause the other critical failure effects listed below.
**Critical Failure** The firearm misfires and also explodes. It becomes [broken](/Conditions.aspx?ID=2), and it deals its normal weapon damage to all creatures in a 20-foot burst centered on the firearm, with a [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=329) Reflex save against your class DC. This damage includes any from the weapon's [fundamental](/Equipment.aspx?Category=23&Subcategory=25) and [property](/Equipment.aspx?Category=23&Subcategory=27) runes.
name: Loose Cannon

</title>

<trait label="Skill" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=144" />

**Prerequisites**
[Alkenstar Agent Dedication](/Feats.aspx?ID=3568); Master in [Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=14)

</column>

---

You're practiced at creating forgeries that establish your outlaw credentials or augment your legal authority. You can [Create a Forgery](/Actions.aspx?ID=60) as an [exploration](/Traits.aspx?ID=66) activity. It takes you about 1 hour per page of the document that you're forging. This time is reduced to 10 minutes per page if you're legendary in Society.
name: Prepare Papers

<title
    level="1"
    right="Feat 10"
    pfs="Limited"
>
[Flash your Badge](/Feats.aspx?ID=3571) <actions string="Two Actions" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Archetype" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=12" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Pathfinder #178: Punks in a Powderkeg](/Sources.aspx?ID=119) pg. 78

**Archetype** [Alkenstar Agent](/Archetypes.aspx?ID=183)

**Prerequisites**
[Alkenstar Agent Dedication](/Feats.aspx?ID=3568); Master in [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=7)

</column>

---

You make a fearsome show of your authority. Roll Intimidation checks to [Demoralize](/Actions.aspx?ID=53) each creature in a 30-foot cone. When you do so, Demoralize gains the [visual](/Traits.aspx?ID=163) trait, and creatures are affected if they can see you. If a target creature hasn't broken a law in the past week (as determined by the GM), the result of your check against that creature is one degree of success worse than the result you rolled.
name: Flash your Badge

markdown:
**Source** [Pathfinder #178: Punks in a Powderkeg](/Sources.aspx?ID=119) pg. 79 **Archetype** [Martial Artist](/Archetypes.aspx?ID=67) **Prerequisites** [Powder Punch Stance](/Feats.aspx?ID=3575) **Requirements** You're in Powder Punch Stance. --- You ignite a pinch of black powder in a foe's face. Choose an adjacent creature. It must attempt a Fortitude save against your class DC. **Critical Success** The creature is unaffected.
**Success** The creature is [dazzled](/Conditions.aspx?ID=7) for 1 round.
**Failure** The creature is [blinded](/Conditions.aspx?ID=1) for 1 round and dazzled for 1 minute. The creature can rub its eyes as an Interact action to end this blinded condition.
**Critical Failure** The creature is blinded for 1 round and dazzled for 1 hour. Black Powder Feats **Source** [Pathfinder #178: Punks in a Powderkeg](/Sources.aspx?ID=119) pg. 55 If the characters agree to Nixbrix's challenge to defeat Bitey or otherwise impress the gang leader, he offers to teach them a few tricks. The characters gain access to the feats in the Black-Powder Berserkers section on page 79, even if they aren't from Alkenstar or the Mana Wastes, though they still must meet the feats' other prerequisites to take them. **Related Feats**: [Inured to Alchemy](/Feats.aspx?ID=3573), [Pain Tolerance](/Feats.aspx?ID=3574), [Powder Punch Stance](/Feats.aspx?ID=3575), [Thunder Clap](/Feats.aspx?ID=3576)
name: Black Powder Flash
requirement_markdown: You're in Powder Punch Stance.

markdown:
<title
    level="1"
    right="Feat 4"
    pfs="Limited"
>
[Inured to Alchemy](/Feats.aspx?ID=3573) <actions string="" />
</title>

<spoilers>May contain spoilers from Outlaws of Alkenstar</spoilers>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Alchemist" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=7" />
<trait label="Barbarian" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=18" />
<trait label="Fighter" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=71" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Pathfinder #178: Punks in a Powderkeg](/Sources.aspx?ID=119) pg. 79

**Prerequisites**
expert in Fortitude saves

**Access**
You're from Alkenstar or the Mana Wastes.

</column>

---

You've developed control over how your body processes alchemical poisons and drugs. Each of your successful saving throws against an [alchemical poison](/Equipment.aspx?Category=6&Subcategory=9), a [drug](/Equipment.aspx?Category=6&Subcategory=47), or an [addiction](/Diseases.aspx?ID=15) reduces the stage by 2, or by 1 for a [virulent](/Traits.aspx?ID=162) alchemical poison, drug, or addiction. Each critical success reduces the stage by 3, or by 2 for a virulent alchemical poison, drug, or addiction.

<title level="2">Black Powder Feats</title>

**Source** [Pathfinder #178: Punks in a Powderkeg](/Sources.aspx?ID=119) pg. 55

If the characters agree to Nixbrix's challenge to defeat Bitey or otherwise impress the gang leader, he offers to teach them a few tricks. The characters gain access to the feats in the Black-Powder Berserkers section on page 79, even if they aren't from Alkenstar or the Mana Wastes, though they still must meet the feats' other prerequisites to take them.

**Related Feats**: [Black Powder Flash](/Feats.aspx?ID=3572), [Pain Tolerance](/Feats.aspx?ID=3574), [Powder Punch Stance](/Feats.aspx?ID=3575), [Thunder Clap](/Feats.aspx?ID=3576)
name: Inured to Alchemy

The characters gain access to the feats in the Black-Powder Berserkers section on page 79, even if they aren't from Alkenstar or the Mana Wastes, though they still must meet the feats' other prerequisites to take them.

**Related Feats**: [Black Powder Flash](/Feats.aspx?ID=3572), [Inured to Alchemy](/Feats.aspx?ID=3573), [Powder Punch Stance](/Feats.aspx?ID=3575), [Thunder Clap](/Feats.aspx?ID=3576)

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Pathfinder #178: Punks in a Powderkeg](/Sources.aspx?ID=119) pg. 79

**Archetype** [Martial Artist](/Archetypes.aspx?ID=67)

**Prerequisites**
[Powder Punch Stance](/Feats.aspx?ID=3575)

**Requirements**
You're in Powder Punch Stance.

</column>

---

You slam your hands together to unleash a deafening blast. Creatures in a 15-foot cone take 3d6 sonic damage, with a [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=329) Fortitude save against your class DC. Creatures that critically fail their save are also [deafened](/Conditions.aspx?ID=8) for 1 minute. You can't use this ability again for 1d4 rounds as your hands recover from the thunderous vibrations.

 At 8th level, and every 2 levels thereafter, the damage from Thunder Clap increases by 1d6.

<title level="2">Black Powder Feats</title>

**Source** [Pathfinder #178: Punks in a Powderkeg](/Sources.aspx?ID=119) pg. 55

If the characters agree to Nixbrix's challenge to defeat Bitey or otherwise impress the gang leader, he offers to teach them a few tricks. The characters gain access to the feats in the Black-Powder Berserkers section on page 79, even if they aren't from Alkenstar or the Mana Wastes, though they still must meet the feats' other prerequisites to take them.

**Related Feats**: [Black Powder Flash](/Feats.aspx?ID=3572), [Inured to Alchemy](/Feats.aspx?ID=3573), [Pain Tolerance](/Feats.aspx?ID=3574), [Powder Punch Stance](/Feats.aspx?ID=3575)
name: Thunder Clap
requirement_markdown: You're in Powder Punch Stance.

<title
    level="1"
    right="Feat 8"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Divine Healing](/Feats.aspx?ID=3577) <actions string="" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Archetype" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=12" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Knights of Lastwall](/Sources.aspx?ID=123) pg. 74

**Archetype** [Knight Vigilant](/Archetypes.aspx?ID=27)

**Prerequisites**
[Knight Vigilant](/Feats.aspx?ID=1092); Expert in [Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=13); worship a deity with a divine font that grants [_heal_](/Spells.aspx?ID=148)

</column>

---

Although you aren't a proper champion of your faith, your deity still recognizes your efforts and provides you with a small divine ability to help others. You gain the [_lay on hands_](/Spells.aspx?ID=395) devotion spell.

 You gain a focus pool of 1 Focus Point or increase the number of Focus Points in your focus pool by 1. You refill your focus pool during your daily preparations, and you regain 1 Focus Point by spending 10 minutes using the [Refocus](/Actions.aspx?ID=71) activity to pray to your deity or perform service toward their causes. The full rules for focus spells are [here](/Rules.aspx?ID=276). You are trained in divine spell attack rolls and spell DCs. Your spellcasting ability is Charisma.

 You must refrain from performing your deity's anathema or lose the ability to cast this spell until you atone.
name: Divine Healing
prerequisite_markdown: [Knight Vigilant](/Feats.aspx?ID=1092); Expert in [Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=13); worship a deity with a divine font that grants [_heal_](/Spells.aspx?ID=148)

Attempt an [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) check against the higher of the triggering creature's Athletics DC or Fortitude DC. **Critical Success** You [disrupt](/Rules.aspx?ID=394) the movement.
**Success** You are able to slow the triggering creature down. It takes a –10-foot circumstance penalty to its Speed for the rest of its triggering movement. This penalty might cause the triggering creature's movement to end immediately based on its affected Speed.
**Failure** As success, but the creature instead takes a –5-foot circumstance penalty to its Speed. Interrupt Charge leads to... [Instinctual Interception](/Feats.aspx?ID=3585)

>
[Emissary of Peace](/Feats.aspx?ID=3580) <actions string="Three Actions" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Archetype" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=12" />
<trait label="Skill" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=144" />
</traits>

**Source** [Knights of Lastwall](/Sources.aspx?ID=123) pg. 74

**Prerequisites**
[Knight Vigilant](/Feats.aspx?ID=1092); Master in [Diplomacy](/Skills.aspx?ID=6)

</column>

---

Even when bloodshed is inevitable, you would rather see your foes surrender. You attempt a Diplomacy check to [Make an Impression](/Actions.aspx?ID=50) against a single foe whose Hit Points are at less than half of their maximum value, followed by an immediate [Request](/Actions.aspx?ID=51) to convince that creature to cease fighting. You take a –5 penalty to your Diplomacy check. The GM sets the DC of the Request based on the circumstances; it's generally at least a very hard DC for the creature's level. Some creatures might simply refuse, and those who agree to parley might find your arguments lacking and start fighting again.
name: Emissary of Peace

</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Archetype" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=12" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Knights of Lastwall](/Sources.aspx?ID=123) pg. 75

**Archetype** [Knight Vigilant](/Archetypes.aspx?ID=27)

**Prerequisites**
[Knight Vigilant](/Feats.aspx?ID=1092)

</column>

---

A knight is nothing without their armor, and you know how to keep yours ready. You can perform a 10-minute ritual, which has the [conjuration](/Traits.aspx?ID=33) and [magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=103) traits, to divert a set of heavy armor you are wearing into an extradimensional space connected to you. While the armor is in this space, you can use a three-action activity, which has the [concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=32), conjuration, and teleportation traits, to phase the armor back and instantly don it. The armor automatically phases away if you don another set of armor, if you become [unconscious](/Conditions.aspx?ID=38), or if you die. If you are incapable of wearing the armor when it phases back, it appears in your square instead.
name: Instant Armor

markdown:
**Source** [Knights of Lastwall](/Sources.aspx?ID=123) pg. 75 **Archetype** [Knight Vigilant](/Archetypes.aspx?ID=27) **Prerequisites** [Knight Vigilant](/Feats.aspx?ID=1092); alignment permitted by the chosen deity --- Unwilling to be outdone by the Crimson Reclaimers and their oath, you've petitioned the gods of good for a sliver of divine magic. Choose one of the following deities whose requirements you meet—for the Any Deity option below, you can select from the following deities or a deity the GM deems appropriate: [Arazni](/Deities.aspx?ID=31), [Arqueros](/Deities.aspx?ID=244), [Falayna](/Deities.aspx?ID=119), [Gendowyn](/Deities.aspx?ID=152), [Iomedae](/Deities.aspx?ID=9), [Kazutal](/Deities.aspx?ID=41), [Kols](/Deities.aspx?ID=89), [Milani](/Deities.aspx?ID=43), [Pharasma](/Deities.aspx?ID=14), [Ragathiel](/Deities.aspx?ID=126), [Sarenrae](/Deities.aspx?ID=16), [Shelyn](/Deities.aspx?ID=17), [Suyuddha](/Deities.aspx?ID=245), [Vildeis](/Deities.aspx?ID=130)—and one of the listed spells. You can Cast that Spell once per day as a 4th-level divine innate spell, and you become trained in divine spell DCs and divine spell attack rolls. At 14th level, the spell heightens to 5th level; at 16th level, the spell heightens to 6th level. Once you've selected a deity and a spell, you can't change this selection. You don't need to worship the deity as your patron deity, as they are willing to grant this boon on behalf of your worthy cause. You must refrain from performing your deity's anathema or lose the ability to cast this spell until you atone.
  • **Any Deity**: [_freedom of movement_](/Spells.aspx?ID=128), [_remove curse_](/Spells.aspx?ID=250), [_resist energy_](/Spells.aspx?ID=256), [_spell immunity_](/Spells.aspx?ID=296), [_status_](/Spells.aspx?ID=308)
  • **Gendowyn**: [_barkskin_](/Spells.aspx?ID=20)
  • **Iomedae**: [_fire shield_](/Spells.aspx?ID=118)
  • [**Irori**](/Deities.aspx?ID=10): [_stoneskin_](/Spells.aspx?ID=312)
  • **Kazutal**: [_mage armor_](/Spells.aspx?ID=176), [_stoneskin_](/Spells.aspx?ID=312)
  • **Milani**: [_fire shield_](/Spells.aspx?ID=118), [_mage armor_](/Spells.aspx?ID=176)
  • **Ragathiel**: [_fire shield_](/Spells.aspx?ID=118)
  • **Sarenrae**: [_wall of fire_](/Spells.aspx?ID=362)
**Special** You can select this feat a second time at 14th level, gaining a second spell to cast once per day from your chosen deity's list or the Any Deity list, and you become an expert in divine spell DCs and divine spell attack rolls. You can select this feat a third time at 18th level. If you do, you gain a third spell to cast once per day from your chosen deity's list or the Any Deity list, and all three spells are heightened to 7th level.
name: Vigilant Benediction
prerequisite_markdown: [Knight Vigilant](/Feats.aspx?ID=1092); alignment permitted by the chosen deity

---

By smearing ash or tainted earth over your skin and armor, you shroud yourself from the dead. You become concealed to [undead](/Traits.aspx?ID=160) using vision or [lifesense](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=21), allowing you to [Hide](/Actions.aspx?ID=62) and [Sneak](/Actions.aspx?ID=63) without other sources of [cover](/Rules.aspx?ID=459) or concealment. This effect lasts only as long as you continue to take no actions other than to Hide, Sneak, or other surreptitious actions. The GM determines which actions end the effect, but attacking, casting spells, activating items, and the like always do.
name: Ashen Veil
prerequisite_markdown: [Knight Reclaimant Dedication](/Feats.aspx?ID=1097); Expert in [Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=13); Master in [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=15)

markdown:
<title
    level="1"
    right="Feat 7"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Voice Cold as Death](/Feats.aspx?ID=3590) <actions string="Single Action" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Archetype" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=12" />
<trait label="Auditory" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=16" />
<trait label="Concentrate" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=32" />
<trait label="Emotion" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=60" />
<trait label="Fear" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=68" />
<trait label="Mental" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=106" />
<trait label="Skill" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=144" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Knights of Lastwall](/Sources.aspx?ID=123) pg. 76

**Archetype** [Knight Reclaimant](/Archetypes.aspx?ID=28)

**Prerequisites**
[Knight Reclaimant Dedication](/Feats.aspx?ID=1097); Master in [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=7)

**Requirements**
You are not [fatigued](/Conditions.aspx?ID=15).

</column>

---

Channeling the energy of the Crimson Oath into your voice, you pronounce a threat in a tone so icy it freezes the blood. Attempt an Intimidation check to [Demoralize](/Actions.aspx?ID=53); you don't suffer a penalty if the target doesn't understand the language you're speaking, but this otherwise functions as normal. You can't use [Intimidating Glare](/Feats.aspx?ID=796) or any other effect that would cause your Demoralize attempt to lose the [auditory](/Traits.aspx?ID=16) trait as part of using this feat. If you critically succeed at your Intimidation check, the target becomes [stunned 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=36), or stunned 2 if the target is [undead](/Traits.aspx?ID=160). After using this feat, you are fatigued until the end of your next turn, and you can't use this feat again until the end of your next turn.
name: Voice Cold as Death
requirement_markdown: You are not [fatigued](/Conditions.aspx?ID=15).

<title
    level="1"
    right="Feat 12"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[For Love, For Lightning](/Feats.aspx?ID=3592) <actions string="" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Rare" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=137" />
<trait label="Archetype" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=12" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Knights of Lastwall](/Sources.aspx?ID=123) pg. 77

**Archetype** [Knight Reclaimant](/Archetypes.aspx?ID=28)

**Prerequisites**
[Knight Reclaimant Dedication](/Feats.aspx?ID=1097)

**Access**
A character who performs a specialized mission for Clarethe Iomedar has access to this option.

</column>

---

Rumors suggest this technique was pioneered by knights who undertook missions for Clarethe Iomedar at the half-moon, but many Crimson Reclaimers have since mastered it. You learn the [_for love, for lightning_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1108) focus spell. If you don't already have one, you gain a focus pool of 1 Focus Point, which you can regain using the [Refocus](/Actions.aspx?ID=71) activity to recite the Crimson Oath and meditate on its teachings; if you already have a focus pool, increase the number of Focus Points in your focus pool by 1. Your knight reclaimant focus spells are divine spells; when you gain this feat, you become trained in divine spell attacks and spell DCs. Charisma is the key spellcasting ability for knight reclaimant focus spells.
name: For Love, For Lightning

markdown:
<title
    level="1"
    right="Feat 12"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Reclaimant Plea](/Feats.aspx?ID=3593) <actions string="" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Archetype" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=12" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Knights of Lastwall](/Sources.aspx?ID=123) pg. 77

**Archetype** [Knight Reclaimant](/Archetypes.aspx?ID=28)

**Prerequisites**
[Knight Reclaimant Dedication](/Feats.aspx?ID=1097); ability to cast divine spells

</column>

---

You call on the power of the Crimson Oath to gain magical aid. Choose one of the following spells: [_air walk_](/Spells.aspx?ID=6), [_dimensional anchor_](/Spells.aspx?ID=70), [_freedom of movement_](/Spells.aspx?ID=128), [_invisibility_](/Spells.aspx?ID=164), [_remove curse_](/Spells.aspx?ID=250), [_searing light_](/Spells.aspx?ID=269). You can Cast that Spell once per day as a 4th-level divine innate spell. At 14th level, the spell heightens to 5th level, and at 16th level, the spell heightens to 6th level. If you anger the mysterious source of the Crimson Oath's power, you lose the ability to cast the spell until you atone.

**Special** You can select this feat a second time at 14th level, gaining a second spell from the list above, [_death ward_](/Spells.aspx?ID=64), or [_flame strike_](/Spells.aspx?ID=120), which you can cast once per day; you also become an expert in divine spell DCs and divine spell attack rolls. You can select this feat a third time at 18th level, gaining either a third spell from the list above, _death ward_, _flame strike_, or [_sunburst_](/Spells.aspx?ID=326), which you can cast once per day; additionally, all three spells from this feat heighten to 7th level, and you become a master of divine spell attacks and spell DCs.

<title level="2">Reclaimant Plea leads to...</title>
[May Death Itself Reconsider](/Feats.aspx?ID=7749)
name: Reclaimant Plea
prerequisite_markdown: [Knight Reclaimant Dedication](/Feats.aspx?ID=1097); ability to cast divine spells

markdown:
**Source** [Knights of Lastwall](/Sources.aspx?ID=123) pg. 77 **Archetype** [Knight Reclaimant](/Archetypes.aspx?ID=28) **Frequency** once per day **Prerequisites** [Knight Reclaimant Dedication](/Feats.aspx?ID=1097); [Invoke the Crimson Oath](/Feats.aspx?ID=1098) --- You've reached the apex of the Crimson Oath's power. The oath's power is overwhelming—too much for a mortal frame—but victory is worth any price. When you use this feat, you cry out the final line of the Crimson Oath, unleashing unbridled power for 1 minute. You can Dismiss this effect. While this effect is active, you gain the following effects:
  • At the beginning of each of your turns, you become [drained 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=68) or increase the value of your drained condition by 1 if you are already drained. You can't reduce the value of your drained condition or remove it while The Tyrant Falls! is active. While The Tyrant Falls! is active, your drained condition does not penalize your Fortitude save.
  • You can cast [_invoke the Crimson Oath_](/Spells.aspx?ID=556) at will without expending Focus Points. When you do, increase the area to a 40-foot cone.
  • You gain resistance to all damage equal to half your character level.
  • Any melee weapon you wield gains the [reach](/Traits.aspx?ID=192) trait if it didn't already possess it, or increases its reach by 5 feet if it already has the reach trait. Your Strikes with that weapon deal an extra 2d6 positive or negative damage (whichever is more effective against the target).
name: The Tyrant Falls!

    pfs="Standard"
>

</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Archetype" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=12" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Prerequisites**
[Lastwall Sentry Dedication](/Feats.aspx?ID=882)

</column>

---

Extensive training against [undead](/Traits.aspx?ID=160) has taught you to cover your weak points, even when you think that you're alone. You aren't [flat-footed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=16) to [hidden](/Conditions.aspx?ID=22), [undetected](/Conditions.aspx?ID=39), or flanking undead of your level or lower. However, the undead can still help their allies flank. If all of your opponents are undead, you gain a +1 circumstance bonus to initiative rolls; the GM determines whether to apply this bonus if undead are disguised as another type of creature, if another type of creature is disguised as undead, or if there are additional opponents you haven't noticed who aren't undead.
name: Always Ready

Your uttered prayers become an aegis, drawing the blessing of your deity across the surface of your shield. Until the beginning of your next turn your shield, any [shield boss](/Weapons.aspx?ID=41), or [shield spikes](/Weapons.aspx?ID=42) gain the effects of a [_ghost touch_](/Equipment.aspx?ID=297) property rune. Additionally, damage you take as a result of an incorporeal creature's Strike can trigger your [Shield Block](/Actions.aspx?ID=1140) reaction, even if the damage isn't physical damage.

prerequisite_markdown: [Lastwall Sentry Dedication](/Feats.aspx?ID=882); you worship a good-aligned deity

Attempt a check to [Recall Knowledge](/Actions.aspx?ID=26) about an undead you can see and then Strike that undead. If your attempt to Recall Knowledge is successful, your Strike treats the target's resistances as 5 lower. The target is then immune to your Practiced Opposition for 24 hours.

---

Regular exposure to necromantic effects left you better able to defend against them. When you have your shield raised, you gain your shield's circumstance bonus to saving throws against effects that would deal negative damage. If you have the [Shield Block](/Actions.aspx?ID=1140) reaction, negative damage you take can trigger the reaction, even though the damage isn't physical damage.
name: Necromantic Bulwark

<title
    level="1"
    right="Feat 10"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Grave Sight](/Feats.aspx?ID=3607) <actions string="" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Archetype" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=12" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Knights of Lastwall](/Sources.aspx?ID=123) pg. 79

**Archetype** [Lastwall Sentry](/Archetypes.aspx?ID=15)

**Prerequisites**
[Lastwall Sentry Dedication](/Feats.aspx?ID=882); [Grave Sense](/Feats.aspx?ID=885)

</column>

---

Your ability to sense undead has improved. You sense undead as an [imprecise](/Rules.aspx?ID=412) sense with a range of 60 feet: an uncomfortable sensation brushes against your spirit and stinks of rot. When in proximity to an undead, you can sense its presence, but it has the [hidden](/Conditions.aspx?ID=22) condition instead of the [observed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=27) condition. An undead using a disguise or otherwise trying to hide its presence must attempt a [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=5) or [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=15) check against your Perception DC to hide its presence from you. If the creature succeeds, it's [undetected](/Conditions.aspx?ID=39), and you have to use the [Seek](/Actions.aspx?ID=84) basic action to detect the creature. As with hearing and other imprecise senses, you still need to Seek to locate an undetected creature.
name: Grave Sight

Whenever you become [drained](/Conditions.aspx?ID=10) or [sickened](/Conditions.aspx?ID=34), reduce the status penalty you take from the condition by 1, to a minimum of 0.

<title level="2">No Stranger to Death leads to...</title>

You hold your shield together with the power of your faith and conviction, refusing to let wood splinter and steel shatter until the day's battles are won. If a shield would be destroyed due to damage taken during your Shield Block, the shield remains intact at 1 Hit Point instead. Its construction is weakened until you repair it, preventing you from using Resolute Defender to save the same shield again until your next daily preparations.

<title
    level="1"
    right="Feat 4"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Angel of Vindication](/Feats.aspx?ID=3612) <actions string="" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Archetype" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=12" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Knights of Lastwall](/Sources.aspx?ID=123) pg. 80

**Archetype** [Assassin](/Archetypes.aspx?ID=48)

**Prerequisites**
[Assassin Dedication](/Feats.aspx?ID=1890)

**Access**
Knights of Lastwall have access to this feat.

**Requirements**
Your mark is an [undead](/Traits.aspx?ID=160) creature.

</column>

---

To aid with your mission of destroying undead, you've specialized in techniques to destroy them, much as other assassins learn to kill the living. When you Strike a [flat-footed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=16) undead foe with a weapon that has the [backstabber](/Traits.aspx?ID=172) weapon trait, you deal 3 extra precision damage instead of 1. If the weapon is a +3 weapon, you deal 6 extra precision damage instead of 2. If you have the [Angel of Death](/Feats.aspx?ID=1893) feat, your attacks also instantly destroy undead at 0 Hit Points, and attempts for the undead to rejuvenate—such as via a [lich's](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=68) _soul cage_ or a [ghost's](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=51) rejuvenation ability—fail unless the effect's [counteract](/Rules.aspx?ID=371) level is higher than half your level when you killed the creature (rounded up), or originates from an [artifact](/Traits.aspx?ID=253) or deity. Finally, if you have the [Assassinate](/Feats.aspx?ID=1894) feat and an undead mark critically fails its save against your Assassinate, it is destroyed.
name: Angel of Vindication
requirement_markdown: Your mark is an [undead](/Traits.aspx?ID=160) creature.

You attempt to [Shove](/Actions.aspx?ID=38) the triggering enemy with your shield just as it attacks, pushing it back just after the attempt. Your shield has the [shove](/Traits.aspx?ID=193) trait for this Shove attempt. If the shove succeeds, you throw off your enemy's timing, and the enemy takes a –1 circumstance penalty on its attack roll against your ally.
name: Drive Back
requirement_markdown: You have your shield raised.

    level="1"
    right="Feat 8"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Preventative Treatment](/Feats.aspx?ID=3618) <actions string="" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Archetype" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=12" />
<trait label="Secret" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=142" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Knights of Lastwall](/Sources.aspx?ID=123) pg. 81

**Archetype** [Medic](/Archetypes.aspx?ID=69)

**Prerequisites**
[Medic Dedication](/Feats.aspx?ID=2027); [Treat Condition](/Feats.aspx?ID=2029)

**Access**
Knights of Lastwall have access to this feat.

</column>

---

You provide preventative remedies and care to help your patient fight off future maladies. You Treat a Condition the target doesn't yet have, naming any condition you could normally treat with Treat Condition. The [counteract](/Rules.aspx?ID=371) check gains the [secret](/Traits.aspx?ID=142) trait, so the GM rolls it secretly. The next time within the next minute the creature would be affected by that condition, your treatment immediately attempts to reduce the condition value with the effects of Treat Condition, using the secret roll result for the check. Preventative Treatment lasts on a creature for 1 minute, until the treatment is used, or until you use Preventative Treatment again on that creature, whichever comes first.
name: Preventative Treatment

    pfs="Standard"
>
[Viking Vindicator](/Feats.aspx?ID=3619) <actions string="" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Archetype" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=12" />

**Prerequisites**
[Viking Dedication](/Feats.aspx?ID=2094)

**Access**
Knights of Lastwall have access to this feat.

</column>

---

You are one of the raiders eagerly planning to launch an assault by sea on the Whispering Tyrant's Isle of Terror. You gain the [Sudden Charge](/Feats.aspx?ID=134) fighter feat, though it's a viking feat for you instead. As normal when gaining an additional feat from an archetype feat, gaining Sudden Charge doesn't count toward fulfilling the special requirement for Viking Dedication. If you already had Sudden Charge, you can retrain it as part of gaining this feat. When you use Sudden Charge to Strike a target in shallow water, the target is [flat-footed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=16) against your Strike, as you use the water kicked up by your passage to camouflage your angle of attack. If you have [Viking Weapon Familiarity](/Feats.aspx?ID=2096) or [Viking Weapon Specialist](/Feats.aspx?ID=2097), add the [bastard sword](/Weapons.aspx?ID=15) and [rapier](/Weapons.aspx?ID=36) to the list of weapons in those feats.
name: Viking Vindicator

<title
    level="1"
    right="Feat 10"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Stalwart Standard](/Feats.aspx?ID=3620) <actions string="" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Archetype" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=12" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Knights of Lastwall](/Sources.aspx?ID=123) pg. 81

**Archetypes** [Cavalier](/Archetypes.aspx?ID=53), [Knight Vigilant](/Archetypes.aspx?ID=27) (Level 10)

**Prerequisites**
[Cavalier's Banner](/Feats.aspx?ID=1920); [Knight Vigilant](/Feats.aspx?ID=1092)

**Access**
Knights of Lastwall have access to this feat.

</column>

---

Your banner makes it harder for your foes to push your allies around and encourages your allies to protect the innocent. Allies benefiting from your Cavalier's Banner feat also gain a +1 circumstance bonus to their Fortitude and Reflex DCs against attempts to [Disarm](/Actions.aspx?ID=41), [Grapple](/Actions.aspx?ID=35), [Shove](/Actions.aspx?ID=38), and [Trip](/Actions.aspx?ID=40). Each benefited ally also gains a +1 status bonus to attack rolls against any creature that has dealt damage to an innocent of a lower level than that ally within the last round; [summoned](/Traits.aspx?ID=154) creatures, companions, or any creatures you or your allies put into danger on purpose to attempt to gain this bonus don't count.
name: Stalwart Standard

</column>

You wave your banner, inspiring allies to throw off the shackles of fear. Each ally presently benefiting from your Cavalier's Banner feat reduces their current [frightened](/Conditions.aspx?ID=19) condition by 1 and can choose to immediately attempt a new saving throw against any one [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=106) effect currently affecting them. Regardless of the result, any ally that attempts a save is temporarily immune to Wave the Flag for 10 minutes.
name: Wave the Flag

</title>

<traits>

<trait label="Champion" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=24" />
<trait label="Fighter" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=71" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Knights of Lastwall](/Sources.aspx?ID=123) pg. 82

**Access**
Knights of Lastwall have access to this feat.

**Requirements**
You are wielding a shield with a [shield boss](/Weapons.aspx?ID=41) or [shield spikes](/Weapons.aspx?ID=42) in one hand.

</column>

---

You change your grip on the shield, allowing you to combine rapid attacks with your shield boss or shield spikes and your main weapon's Strikes in a series of swift motions. You reduce your shield boss and shield spikes weapon damage die to 1d4. As long as the weapon damage die is 1d4, your shield boss and shield spike Strikes gain the [agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=170) weapon trait. You can use Agile Shield Grip again to switch to a normal grip, returning the damage to the usual amount and removing the agile trait.
name: Agile Shield Grip

You developed a method to chemically purify and consecrate your bombs on the fly. You include this additive mixture when crafting your alchemist's fire or ghost charge. The created bomb deals 1d4 [persistent positive damage](/Conditions.aspx?ID=29) in addition to whatever damage it would normally cause. As usual for positive damage, this damage harms only [undead](/Traits.aspx?ID=160) and creatures with [negative healing](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=42).

<trait label="Champion" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=24" />
<trait label="Fighter" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=71" />
</traits>

**Access**

</column>

---

You attack carefully to knock some sense into a creature whose actions aren't their own. Make a Strike against a [confused](/Conditions.aspx?ID=5) ally. The Strike is [nonlethal](/Traits.aspx?ID=188) and doesn't take a –2 penalty for using a weapon without the nonlethal trait; if you hit and deal damage, your ally automatically succeeds at the flat check to remove the confused condition when taking damage.
name: Knock Sense

<traits>

Your spell becomes volatile and explosive. If the next action you use is to Cast a Spell that deals damage to a single target and the spell successfully damages that target, the spell explodes, dealing [splash](/Traits.aspx?ID=150) damage equal to the level of the spell cast to adjacent creatures. Unlike normally, this splash damage doesn't apply to the target. The splash damage dealt is of the same type the spell deals.

<title
    level="1"
    right="Feat 6"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Divine Emissary](/Feats.aspx?ID=3633) <actions string="" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Sorcerer" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=148" />
<trait label="Witch" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=323" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Knights of Lastwall](/Sources.aspx?ID=123) pg. 83

**Prerequisites**
a [familiar](Familiars.aspx), you follow a good-aligned deity or patron

**Access**
Knights of Lastwall have access to this feat.

</column>

---

Your familiar is a divine emissary, sent to you by your deity or patron and infused with celestial powers. You can select one additional familiar ability each day, which must be one of the following divine emissary familiar abilities. You can't select more than one divine emissary familiar ability at a time.

 **Erudite** Your familiar has incredible religious knowledge. Despite being a minion, your familiar gains 1 reaction at the start of its turns, which it can use only to [Aid](/Actions.aspx?ID=75) you on a [Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=13) check (it still has to prepare to help you as normal for the Aid reaction). It automatically succeeds its check to Aid you with Religion checks or automatically critically succeeds if you're a master in Religion.

 **Luminous** Your familiar glows with a comforting light. It sheds bright light in a 30-foot radius and dim light for the next 30 feet. It can suppress or resume this light by using an action, which has the [concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=32) trait.

 **Medic** Your familiar can draw upon the power of your deity to Cast a Spell once per day. The familiar can cast the 1-action version of [_heal_](/Spells.aspx?ID=148) at a level 2 levels lower than your highest-level spell slot, which it can cast only on you. You must be able to cast 3rd-level spells from spell slots to select this ability.

 **Radiant** Your familiar is filled with a divine radiance that makes it resistant to the forces of evil. Your familiar gains resistance to evil and [negative](/Traits.aspx?ID=118) damage equal to half your level.
name: Divine Emissary
prerequisite_markdown: a [familiar](Familiars.aspx), you follow a good-aligned deity or patron

<title
    level="1"
    right="Feat 6"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Shield Wall](/Feats.aspx?ID=3634) <actions string="Reaction" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Champion" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=24" />
<trait label="Fighter" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=71" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Knights of Lastwall](/Sources.aspx?ID=123) pg. 84

**Access**
Knights of Lastwall have access to this feat.

**Trigger**
Your shield is raised and an adjacent ally [Raises a Shield](/Actions.aspx?ID=98).

</column>

---

You use your shield in tandem with an ally's, forming a barricade. Both you and the triggering creature have [standard cover](/Rules.aspx?ID=459) as long as you remain adjacent to each other and you both have your shields raised; when this is no longer the case, the effect ends. You and your ally can't use this cover to [Hide](/Actions.aspx?ID=62), as it's obvious you're standing there holding your shields, but you and your ally can use it to [Take Cover](/Actions.aspx?ID=90). Normally, if you and your ally don't move, Shield Wall lasts until the beginning of your next turn, when your shield is no longer raised.
name: Shield Wall

You twist your hand in front of the blow, ensuring the enemy's attack shatters the required _holy water_ or bomb. The creature takes the on-hit and [splash](/Traits.aspx?ID=150) damage from the _holy water_ or required bomb, not counting any additional effects you would add or apply when you throw the bomb, such as weapon specialization or [Calculated Splash](/Feats.aspx?ID=99). ---

You infuse a spell with the power of your faith, consecrating it. If the next action you use is to Cast a Spell that targets a single [undead](/Traits.aspx?ID=160), you can expend a Focus Point, channeling the power of your focus spells into the primary spell. If you do, the spell you cast deals additional good or positive damage (your choice) equal to the level of your focus spells. As normal for additional damage, this additional damage is doubled if the spell cast requires an attack roll and the result of the attack roll is a critical hit, or if the spell cast requires a saving throw and the result of the saving throw is a critical failure.
name: Consecrate Spell
prerequisite_markdown: ability to cast focus spells, [divine](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=2) spells

<title
    level="1"
    right="Feat 10"
    pfs="Limited"
>
[Glass Skin](/Feats.aspx?ID=3644) <actions string="" />
</title>

<spoilers>May contain spoilers from Outlaws of Alkenstar</spoilers>

<traits>
<trait label="Rare" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=137" />
<trait label="Abjuration" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=2" />
<trait label="Aftermath" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=424" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Pathfinder #180: The Smoking Gun](/Sources.aspx?ID=125) pg. 77

**Prerequisites**
You were present at the death of the [medusa](/Monsters.aspx?ID=297) Alethsia, whose vitrumantic powers were passed on to you in the wake of her destruction.

</column>

---

Even if vitrumancy is a mystery to you, your blood and flesh know the secrets of the art of arcane glass. Patches of your skin to flicker into glass without warning. By focusing the effect, you can concentrate the glass into a powerful protective layer of magical obsidian. Once per day, you can cast [_stoneskin_](/Spells.aspx?ID=312) on yourself as an innate primal spell, though the spell covers you in a layer of glass instead of stone. During this innate stoneskin, you gain the Shatter Glass reaction. At 18th level, the stoneskin is heightened to 6th level instead of 4th.

**Shatter Glass**<actions string="Reaction" /> **Trigger** An adjacent creature Strikes you with a melee weapon or [unarmed](/Traits.aspx?ID=199) attack and deals damage; **Requirements** Your innate _stoneskin_ from Glass Skin is active; **Effect** You shatter some of the glass on your skin to damage your attacker. The attacker takes 3d10 damage and the duration of the _stoneskin_ decreases by 1 minute. At 14th level, the damage increases to 4d10, and at 18th level, the damage increases to 5d10.
name: Glass Skin
prerequisite_markdown: You were present at the death of the [medusa](/Monsters.aspx?ID=297) Alethsia, whose vitrumantic powers were passed on to you in the wake of her destruction.

---

You can animate corpses through cunning engineering. You gain a [prototype construct companion](/Rules.aspx?ID=1600) to serve as a minion. Unlike normal construct companions, yours is a corpse animated by clockwork gears and technological prowess.

**Special** You can't select another dedication feat until you've gained two other feats from the clockwork reanimator archetype. If you retrain out of this feat, you lose the rituals learned from it.

[Advanced Reanimated Companion](/Feats.aspx?ID=3646), [Hijack Undead](/Feats.aspx?ID=3647), [Improved Hijack Undead](/Feats.aspx?ID=3651), [It's Alive!](/Feats.aspx?ID=3648), [Self-Destruct](/Feats.aspx?ID=3650), [Unwind Death](/Feats.aspx?ID=3653), [Zombie Horde](/Feats.aspx?ID=3654)

Make a melee Strike against an adjacent undead creature. On a success, instead of taking damage, the target becomes [controlled](/Conditions.aspx?ID=6) by you if its level is equal to or lower than your level – 3. It can attempt a Will saving throw against the higher of your class DC or spell DC to resist being controlled by you. If the target is already under someone else's command, the controlling creature also rolls a saving throw, and the undead uses the better result. **Critical Success** The target is unaffected and temporarily immune for 24 hours.
**Success** The target is unaffected.
**Failure** The undead creature becomes a [minion](/Traits.aspx?ID=109) under your control for 1 minute. The control ends if you or an ally attacks the minion undead.
**Critical Failure** As failure, but the duration is 1 hour. Hijack Undead leads to... [Improved Hijack Undead](/Feats.aspx?ID=3651)

Attempt a DC 40 [Crafting](/Skills.aspx?ID=4) check to revive a dead creature that has been dead for no more than 3 rounds. If you succeed, the target returns to life with the effects of [_raise dead_](/Spells.aspx?ID=243), except it still has the [wounded](/Conditions.aspx?ID=42) condition it had before dying, increased by 1 (or wounded 1 if it wasn't wounded before dying). Whether you succeed or fail, the target is then temporarily immune to Unwind Death for 1 day.

You Command your reanimated construct companion. Instead of its normal actions, the companion splits into a horde of Small and Tiny construct companions within a 30-foot emanation from its original space. The horde makes a Strike against each foe within the emanation with a +2 circumstance bonus to its attack rolls. The multiple attack penalty doesn't increase until after the companion makes all the attacks. The miniature companions recombine into a single companion of their original size at any location within the emanation.

[Counter Thought](/Feats.aspx?ID=3657) <actions string="Reaction" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Abjuration" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=2" />
<trait label="Occult" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=120" />
<trait label="Psychic" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=430" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Dark Archive](/Sources.aspx?ID=129) pg. 24

**Trigger**
A creature Casts a Spell with the [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=106) trait.

**Requirements**
You have an unexpended spell slot you could use to Cast a Spell with the mental trait.

</column>

---

When a foe Casts a Spell that has the mental trait and you can see its manifestations, you can use your own mental magic to disrupt it. You expend one of your spell slots to counter the triggering creature's casting of a spell with the mental trait. You lose your spell slot as if you had cast the triggering spell; this spell slot must be one for which you could Cast a Spell with the mental trait. You then attempt to [counteract](/Rules.aspx?ID=371) the triggering spell.
name: Counter Thought
requirement_markdown: You have an unexpended spell slot you could use to Cast a Spell with the mental trait.

<title
    level="1"
    right="Feat 1"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Mental Buffer](/Feats.aspx?ID=3658) <actions string="" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Psychic" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=430" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Dark Archive](/Sources.aspx?ID=129) pg. 24

</column>

---

Your mind deflects some amount of harm from attacks that induce pain or manipulate your mind directly. You gain resistance to mental damage equal to half your level (minimum 1). This resistance increases to equal to your level while your [Psyche is Unleashed](/Actions.aspx?ID=1222).
name: Mental Buffer

[Psychic Rapport](/Feats.aspx?ID=3659) <actions string="" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Psychic" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=430" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Dark Archive](/Sources.aspx?ID=129) pg. 24

</column>

---

Your telepathic sensitivity intensifies when you're affected by mental magic, making it easier for you to link to other creatures. After you Cast a Spell with the [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=106) trait, until the start of your next turn, you gain a +1 circumstance bonus to [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=5), [Diplomacy](/Skills.aspx?ID=6), and [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=7) checks, and to Perception checks to [Sense Motive](/Actions.aspx?ID=85).
name: Psychic Rapport

<title
    level="1"
    right="Feat 2"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Mental Balm](/Feats.aspx?ID=3660) <actions string="" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Amp" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=425" />
<trait label="Emotion" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=60" />
<trait label="Mental" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=106" />
<trait label="Psychic" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=430" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Dark Archive](/Sources.aspx?ID=129) pg. 24

</column>

---

Your spells release soothing mental waves. Use this amp in place of a psi cantrip's normal amp entry. You can use this amp only on a psi cantrip that targets or affects you or one or more of your allies and doesn't target or affect any enemies.

---
**Amp** You or one ally within 30 feet gains a +2 status bonus to Will saves against emotion effects for 1 minute. You can also have the amped psi cantrip attempt to counteract one effect imposing the frightened condition on yourself or the chosen ally, or an effect imposing the stupefied condition that has a duration of 1 hour or less. If you successfully counteract the effect, you remove only the frightened and stupefied conditions, not any other part of the effect.
name: Mental Balm

</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Amp" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=425" />

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Dark Archive](/Sources.aspx?ID=129) pg. 24

</column>

---

You use your magic to bend space, causing your spell to strike from a strange vector. Use this amp in place of a psi cantrip's normal amp entry. The amped psi cantrip must have a range.

---
**Amp** Choose a square within 30 feet to which you have line of effect and line of sight. Determine your spell's line of effect and line of sight from there, as well as whether creatures have cover against the spell. The spell can exceed its normal range as measured from you, as long as it's within its normal range from the square you chose.
name: Warp Space

<title
    level="1"
    right="Feat 4"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Astral Tether](/Feats.aspx?ID=3663) <actions string="Single Action" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Abjuration" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=2" />
<trait label="Occult" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=120" />
<trait label="Psychic" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=430" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Dark Archive](/Sources.aspx?ID=129) pg. 24

</column>

---

You spin out a thread of psychic energy that connects you to an ally, using it as a conduit for your abilities. Choose a willing creature within 60 feet and connect to it. Whenever you would gain a benefit from a psychic amp, you can have the amp affect the tethered creature instead. You can do so only if the amp grants a distinct benefit, not if it alters the amped spell. 

The tether can't be severed physically but breaks if the distance between you and the tethered ally ever exceeds 60 feet, if you become [unconscious](/Conditions.aspx?ID=38), or if you use Astral Tether again.
name: Astral Tether

    pfs="Standard"
>
[Homing Beacon](/Feats.aspx?ID=3664) <actions string="" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Amp" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=425" />
<trait label="Psychic" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=430" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Dark Archive](/Sources.aspx?ID=129) pg. 24

</column>

---

As your spell strikes a target, you plant a beacon in the target's mind that announces its presence to those nearby. Use this amp in place of a psi cantrip's normal amp entry. The amped cantrip must be one that has one or more targets and must either require a spell attack roll or have a saving throw.

---
**Amp** Choose one creature hit by the spell (if the spell has a spell attack roll) or that fails its save against the spell (if the spell requires a save). A psychic beacon emits from the creature's location, making it easier to notice the creature as the mind and attention of onlookers is subtly drawn to it. For 1 minute, if the target would be [invisible](/Conditions.aspx?ID=26), it's instead merely [concealed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=4), and if it would be concealed, it's no longer concealed.
name: Homing Beacon

---

You siphon residual psychic energies from your spell into one weapon you're wielding or one of your unarmed attacks and when you unleash your mind, the energies flare to match. The attack deals an extra 1d6 force damage until the end of the current turn. If your Psyche is Unleashed, this benefit instead lasts until your psyche subsides.

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Dark Archive](/Sources.aspx?ID=129) pg. 25

</column>

---

You can create a prototypical creature from the depths of your imagination instead of calling one from elsewhere. Whenever you summon a creature, you can choose to instead create a psychic construct of that creature. This grants it resistance to physical damage equal to half its level, weakness 5 to force and mental damage, and the ability to pass through enemies' spaces without needing to [Tumble Through](/Actions.aspx?ID=29) (though it can't end its turn in an occupied space).
name: Thoughtform Summoning

 At 5th level and every 2 levels thereafter, the damage increases by 1d6.

    pfs="Standard"
>
[Parallel Breakthrough](/Feats.aspx?ID=3669) <actions string="" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Psychic" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=430" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Dark Archive](/Sources.aspx?ID=129) pg. 25

</column>

---

You expand your mind beyond your area of psychic specialty. You gain a 1st-level psi cantrip for a conscious mind other than your own; this can be either the unique surface cantrip or one of the common cantrips and the accompanying amp. If you choose a common cantrip, you also gain the special benefit granted by that conscious mind when casting the cantrip without an amp. You don't gain any other benefits of that conscious mind, such as additional spells known.
name: Parallel Breakthrough

<title
    level="1"
    right="Feat 6"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Sixth Sense](/Feats.aspx?ID=3670) <actions string="" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Divination" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=47" />
<trait label="Occult" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=120" />
<trait label="Psychic" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=430" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Dark Archive](/Sources.aspx?ID=129) pg. 26

</column>

---

Your psychic abilities also allow you to detect the lingering thoughts of spirits and similar entities. While you're exploring but not [Searching](/Actions.aspx?ID=519), the GM rolls a secret check for you to find [haunts](/Traits.aspx?ID=88) that usually require Searching, as well as [spirits](/Traits.aspx?ID=149), creatures on the [Ethereal Plane](/Planes.aspx?ID=9), and beings made entirely of spiritual essence, such as [celestials](/Traits.aspx?ID=23), [fiends](/Traits.aspx?ID=70), and [monitors](/Traits.aspx?ID=111).

 You can also potentially notice [ethereal](/Equipment.aspx?ID=284) creatures and spirits inside solid objects, provided they are within 30 feet of you. This applies while Searching, [Seeking](/Actions.aspx?ID=84), and on the automatic secret check from Sixth Sense. You can still notice spirits only on a successful check, and you can't see them if they're more than 5 feet inside an object.

 When you notice a creature with your Sixth Sense, you also learn its location, making it [hidden](/Conditions.aspx?ID=22) to you if it had been [undetected](/Conditions.aspx?ID=39).
name: Sixth Sense

markdown:
**Source** [Dark Archive](/Sources.aspx?ID=129) pg. 26 **Trigger** You [Unleash your Psyche](/Actions.aspx?ID=1222). --- When you Unleash your Psyche, all your rage and pain—the portion of your psyche dedicated to cruel retribution—come along with it. The force of your dark persona's negativity batters constantly against all creatures in a 30-foot emanation when you Unleash your Psyche and for as long as your Psyche is Unleashed. A creature must attempt a Will save against your spell DC the first time it enters the emanation, or if it's in the emanation when you take this action; it doesn't need to attempt a save again, even if it leaves the emanation and returns. A creature frightened by your Dark Persona's Presence can't decrease its frightened value below 1 while within the emanation. **Critical Success** The creature is unaffected.
**Success** The target is [frightened](/Conditions.aspx?ID=76) 1.
**Failure** The target is frightened 2.
**Critical Failure** The target is frightened 3. While your Psyche is Unleashed, your dark persona cares only for destruction. You can Cast a Spell only if it can directly damage an enemy or object or can impose a detrimental condition or penalty on one.
name: Dark Persona's Presence

The force of your dark persona's negativity batters constantly against all creatures in a 30-foot emanation when you Unleash your Psyche and for as long as your Psyche is Unleashed.
</summary>

    right="Feat 8"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Mental Static](/Feats.aspx?ID=3674) <actions string="Reaction" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Enchantment" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=61" />
<trait label="Mental" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=106" />
<trait label="Nonlethal" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=188" />
<trait label="Occult" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=120" />
<trait label="Psychic" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=430" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Dark Archive](/Sources.aspx?ID=129) pg. 27

**Trigger**
You roll a critical success on a Will save against a [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=106) effect from a creature.

</column>

---

Your thoughts sting back when a lesser mind tries to invade your own. You deal mental damage equal to your level to the triggering creature. If you so desire, you can also send the creature a single mental image to coincide with the damage, such as a watchful eye or the dread of floating in an infinite sea.
name: Mental Static

<title
    level="1"
    right="Feat 8"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Remove Presence](/Feats.aspx?ID=3675) <actions string="" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Amp" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=425" />
<trait label="Illusion" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=92" />
<trait label="Mental" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=106" />
<trait label="Occult" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=120" />
<trait label="Psychic" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=430" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Dark Archive](/Sources.aspx?ID=129) pg. 27

</column>

---

Your spell reaches into the mind of a creature and removes you from it. The amped cantrip must be one that has one or more targets and must either require a spell attack roll or have a saving throw. Use this amp in place of the psi cantrip's normal amp entry.

---
**Amp** Choose one target of the spell or one creature in its area. If that enemy fails its save or the spell hits it, you become [undetected](/Conditions.aspx?ID=39) by that creature, disappearing from its senses for 1 minute or until you use a [hostile action](/Rules.aspx?ID=300). This is similar to being [invisible](/Conditions.aspx?ID=26), but effects like [_see invisibility_](/Spells.aspx?ID=271) don't reveal you—you're affecting the target's mind, not its vision. [_True seeing_](/Spells.aspx?ID=344) can still see through this illusion if the [counteract](/Rules.aspx?ID=371) check succeeds.
name: Remove Presence

markdown:
<title
    level="1"
    right="Feat 8"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Thoughtsense](/Feats.aspx?ID=3676) <actions string="" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Divination" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=47" />
<trait label="Mental" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=106" />
<trait label="Occult" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=120" />
<trait label="Psychic" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=430" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Dark Archive](/Sources.aspx?ID=129) pg. 27

</column>

---

You can detect the flickers of mental activity let off by all thinking creatures. You gain thoughtsense as a [vague](/Rules.aspx?ID=413) sense that has a range of 30 feet—like all vague senses, it's only about as precise as an average human's sense of smell, meaning you generally can predict only if a creature is present. Your thoughtsense detects only thinking creatures, so a creature that is unthinking or otherwise immune to mental effects (such as many constructs and oozes) can't be perceived using your thoughtsense, regardless of how precise it is. 

While your [Psyche is Unleashed](/Actions.aspx?ID=1222), your thoughtsense upgrades to an imprecise [sense](/Rules.aspx?ID=412), letting you use it to [Seek](/Actions.aspx?ID=84) out creatures.
name: Thoughtsense

<title
    level="1"
    right="Feat 8"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Whispering Steps](/Feats.aspx?ID=3677) <actions string="" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Amp" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=425" />
<trait label="Enchantment" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=61" />
<trait label="Mental" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=106" />
<trait label="Occult" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=120" />
<trait label="Psychic" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=430" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Dark Archive](/Sources.aspx?ID=129) pg. 27

</column>

---

Your spell leaves a lingering connection between you and a creature—one through which you can nudge the creature's mind this way or that. The amped cantrip must take 2 or more actions to cast, target one or more creatures, and either require a spell attack roll or have a saving throw. Use this amp in place of the psi cantrip's normal amp entry.

---
**Amp** Choose one enemy who is a target of the spell. If that enemy fails its save or the spell hits it, you whisper in the creature's mind, forcing it to Step in a direction of your choosing; as this is [forced movement](/Rules.aspx?ID=451), you can't force the creature to Step into hazardous terrain, off a ledge, or the like. After it Steps, the creature becomes temporarily immune to this amp for 24 hours.
name: Whispering Steps

<title
    level="1"
    right="Feat 10"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Dream Guise](/Feats.aspx?ID=3678) <actions string="Single Action" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Illusion" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=92" />
<trait label="Occult" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=120" />
<trait label="Psyche" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=429" />
<trait label="Psychic" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=430" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Dark Archive](/Sources.aspx?ID=129) pg. 27

**Prerequisites**
[wandering reverie](/SubconsciousMinds.aspx?ID=4) subconscious mind

</column>

---

You Stride into a willing ally's space, at which point both of your appearances shift into a shared third appearance, usually one that looks like a mix of the two of you. Then, either you or your ally Steps. You each maintain the merged appearance. Creatures who are observing this know what happened, but still must [Seek](/Actions.aspx?ID=84) or otherwise engage with the illusion to attempt to disbelieve it and determine which of you is which. Otherwise, they can't tell the difference and have an equal chance to target each of you (DC 11 flat check). This illusion ends as soon as either you or the ally you're merged with acts.
name: Dream Guise

</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Psychic" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=430" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Dark Archive](/Sources.aspx?ID=129) pg. 28

**Trigger**
A non-[minion](/Traits.aspx?ID=109) ally within 60 feet is reduced to 0 Hit Points.

</column>

---

The shock of seeing your ally near death breaks whatever limits exist in your mind, sending your power spilling forth. If your psyche is currently unleashed, you can immediately use a [psyche](/Traits.aspx?ID=429) action that takes 1 action or less to use. If your psyche is not currently unleashed, you [Unleash your Psyche](/Actions.aspx?ID=1222), which remains unleashed until the end of your next turn. You can ignore Unleash Psyche's requirement of needing to have Cast a Spell on your previous turn, but you still can't Unleash your Psyche if you're [stupefied](/Conditions.aspx?ID=37) or for 2 rounds after your psyche subsides.
name: No!!!

Your magic saturates your surroundings with hateful psychic energy. Use this amp in place of a psi cantrip's normal amp entry. The cantrip must be one that takes 2 or more actions to cast and targets a creature.

---
**Amp** Choose one target of the spell. Psychic phenomena turn themselves on the target—typically, objects fling themselves at the creature. At the beginning of each of the target's turns, it takes 1d4 bludgeoning damage for each spell level of the psi cantrip this amp was added to, with a [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=329) Reflex save. The target remains marked by psychic ire for 3 rounds. The effect persists even if you fall unconscious or leave the area.
name: Target of Psychic Ire

    pfs="Standard"
>
[Cranial Detonation](/Feats.aspx?ID=3692) <actions string="Free Action" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Death" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=40" />
<trait label="Mindshift" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=428" />
<trait label="Psyche" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=429" />

</traits>

**Frequency**
once per round

**Trigger**
You successfully reduce one or more non-[mindless](/Traits.aspx?ID=108) enemies to 0 Hit Points with a spell.

</column>

---

As your overwhelming psyche fells a creature, you use its flickering consciousness to detonate psychic energy from the creature's head. Each enemy you reduced to 0 Hit Points dies and its head explodes. Each exploding head generates a shockwave in a 15-foot emanation around that enemy. Each creature in any of the emanations takes 10d6 bludgeoning damage with a [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=329) Reflex save. If this damage reduces an enemy to 0 Hit Points, its head also explodes, potentially damaging more creatures and potentially causing more detonations. A given creature can take damage only once from a single use of Cranial Detonation.
name: Cranial Detonation

**Source** [Dark Archive](/Sources.aspx?ID=129) pg. 29 --- You shed some of your material form, becoming a being of pure thought. This has the following effects.
  • You gain resistance 10 to physical damage but weakness 5 to mental damage and weakness 10 to any effect that deals damage by targeting the spirit, such as [_spirit blast_](/Spells.aspx?ID=301).
  • You can change your appearance on a whim. This takes a single action, which has the [concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=32) trait, has the effects of [_illusory disguise_](/Spells.aspx?ID=159), and lasts until you change your appearance again.
  • Once per year, when you die, you automatically return to life the next day as long as one living sentient creature on your plane remembers you; the memories enable you to reconstruct yourself from their thoughts. You appear in the vicinity of the creature that remembers you most strongly.
name: Become Thought

Your wandering studies mean you've heard rumors or theories about almost every topic... though admittedly, your sources aren't always the most reliable. You can take a –2 penalty to your check to [Recall Knowledge](/Actions.aspx?ID=26) with [Esoteric Lore](/Skills.aspx?ID=8) to Recall Knowledge about any topic, not just the usual topics available for Esoteric Lore. Additionally, when you succeed at your check to [Exploit a Vulnerability](/Actions.aspx?ID=1227), compare the result of your Esoteric Lore check to the DC to Recall Knowledge for that creature; if that number would be a success or a critical success, you gain information as if you had succeeded at the Recall Knowledge check.
name: Diverse Lore

**Source** [Dark Archive](/Sources.aspx?ID=129) pg. 42 --- From your collection of religious trinkets, you pull out opposing divine objects—such as the religious symbols of two deities that are hated enemies—and combine them in a display that causes discordant clashes of divine energy that are especially distracting to the faithful. Roll your choice of a [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=5) or [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=7) check against the Will DC of a creature you can see within 60 feet, with the following results. If the creature is particularly devoted to a deity, such as a [cleric](/Classes.aspx?ID=5), [celestial](/Traits.aspx?ID=23), [monitor](/Traits.aspx?ID=111), [fiend](/Traits.aspx?ID=70), or other creature with [divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=48) spells, you gain a +2 circumstance bonus to your skill check. The GM might determine that a creature that despises all deities, such as an [alghollthu](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=1), is unaffected. **Critical Success** The creature is [flat-footed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=16) to your attacks until the end of your next turn.
**Success** The creature is flat-footed against your attacks until the end of your current turn.
name: Divine Disharmony

>

</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Esoterica" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=427" />
<trait label="Manipulate" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=104" />

</column>

---

Marigold, spider lily, pennyroyal—many primal traditions connect flowers and plants with the boundary between life and death, and you can leverage this association to keep an ally on this side of the line. You place a small plant or similar symbol on an adjacent [dying](/Conditions.aspx?ID=11) creature, immediately stabilizing them; the creature is no longer dying and is instead [unconscious](/Conditions.aspx?ID=38) at 0 Hit Points.

 If you spend 2 actions instead of 1, you empower the act further by uttering a quick folk blessing to chase away ongoing pain, adding the [auditory](/Traits.aspx?ID=16) trait to the action. When you do so, attempt flat checks to remove each source of [persistent damage](/Conditions.aspx?ID=29) affecting the target; due to the particularly effective assistance, the DC is 10 instead of the usual 15.
name: Root to Life

---

Your multidisciplinary study of magic means you know how to activate the magic in [scrolls](/Equipment.aspx?Category=15&Subcategory=52) with ease. You can activate scrolls of any magical tradition, using your thaumaturge class DC for the scroll's DC, rather than a particular spell DC. If a spell is on the spell list for multiple traditions, you choose which tradition to use at the time you activate the scroll. You can draw and activate scrolls with the same hand holding an implement, much like you can for esoterica.

<title level="2">Scroll Thaumaturgy leads to...</title>
[Scroll Esoterica](/Feats.aspx?ID=3713)

You can tug on the bonds of ownership between yourself and your [implement](Implements.aspx), causing it to find its way back to you. You look down and find that your implement has mysteriously appeared in your free hand, as long as the implement was within 1 mile and on the same plane of existence. If your implement is attended by another creature, that creature can prevent the implement from teleporting away if it succeeds at a Will save against your class DC. If the creature succeeds, you can't attempt to Call that Implement again; you must track the creature down to get the implement back.
name: Call Implement

<trait label="Thaumaturge" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=432" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Dark Archive](/Sources.aspx?ID=129) pg. 42

**Prerequisites**
[Exploit Vulnerability](/Actions.aspx?ID=1227)

</column>

---

When you apply antithetical material against a creature successfully, you also ward yourself against its next attacks. When you succeed at your check to Exploit a Vulnerability, you gain a +1 status bonus to your AC against the creature's next attack and a +1 status bonus to your next saving throw against the creature; if you critically succeed, these bonuses are +2 instead. You can gain these bonuses only once per day against a particular creature, and the benefit ends if you Exploit Vulnerability again.

<title level="2">Esoteric Warden leads to...</title>
[Shared Warding](/Feats.aspx?ID=3722)
name: Esoteric Warden

</summary>

markdown:
<title
    level="1"
    right="Feat 2"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Talisman Esoterica](/Feats.aspx?ID=3706) <actions string="" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Esoterica" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=427" />
<trait label="Thaumaturge" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=432" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Dark Archive](/Sources.aspx?ID=129) pg. 43

</column>

---

You know how to assemble the supernatural objects in your esoterica into a number of temporary [talismans](/Equipment.aspx?Category=15&Subcategory=19). Each day during your daily preparations, you can make two talismans with an item level no higher than half your level. You must know each talisman's formula. A talisman created this way is a temporary item and loses its magic the next time you make your daily preparations.

You know the formulas for all common talismans in the _Core Rulebook_ of your level or lower. You remember your talisman formulas through oral tradition or mnemonics, so you don't need a [formula book](/Equipment.aspx?ID=22) for them.

<title level="2">Talisman Esoterica leads to...</title>
[Elaborate Talisman Esoterica](/Feats.aspx?ID=3716), [Grand Talisman Esoterica](/Feats.aspx?ID=3725)
name: Talisman Esoterica

    level="1"
    right="Feat 2"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Turn Away Misfortune](/Feats.aspx?ID=3707) <actions string="Reaction" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Abjuration" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=2" />
<trait label="Esoterica" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=427" />
<trait label="Fortune" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=76" />
<trait label="Manipulate" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=104" />
<trait label="Occult" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=120" />
<trait label="Thaumaturge" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=432" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Dark Archive](/Sources.aspx?ID=129) pg. 43

**Trigger**
You would attempt a roll affected by a [misfortune](/Traits.aspx?ID=110) effect.

</column>

---

You perform a superstition, such as casting salt over your shoulder to ward off bad luck. Turn Away Misfortune's [fortune](/Traits.aspx?ID=76) trait cancels out the misfortune effect, causing you to roll normally. As normal, you can apply only one fortune ability to a roll, so if you Turned Away Misfortune on an attack roll, you couldn't also use an ability like [Halfling Luck](/Feats.aspx?ID=54) to alter the roll further.
name: Turn Away Misfortune

    pfs="Standard"

[Breached Defenses](/Feats.aspx?ID=3708) <actions string="" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Thaumaturge" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=432" />

<column gap="tiny">
