[Exploit Vulnerability](/Actions.aspx?ID=1227)

</column>

---

You can find the one weak point in a creature's scales, wards, or armor to get past its resistances. When you succeed at Exploit Vulnerability, you learn about the highest of the creature's resistances that can be bypassed (for example, if the creature has resistance to physical damage except silver), if the creature has one. If you prefer, you can choose the following benefit instead of one of the usual two benefits from Exploit Vulnerability.

 **Breached Defenses** You can choose this benefit only if you succeeded at Exploit Vulnerability and learned the creature has at least one resistance that can be bypassed. Choose one such resistance. Your unarmed and weapon Strikes bypass the chosen resistance.
name: Breached Defenses

<title
    level="1"
    right="Feat 4"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Paired Link](/Feats.aspx?ID=3710) <actions string="" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Divination" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=47" />
<trait label="Esoterica" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=427" />
<trait label="Fortune" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=76" />
<trait label="Occult" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=120" />
<trait label="Thaumaturge" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=432" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Dark Archive](/Sources.aspx?ID=129) pg. 44

</column>

---

You break a trinket such as a lodestone or jade pendant in two, creating a sympathetic link between the halves that bridges distance. During your daily preparations, you perform a short ceremony where you gift one of the two halves to a willing ally. On that ally, you can cast spells and use thaumaturge abilities that have a range of touch at a range of 30 feet instead, and the ally can cast spells that have a range of touch on you from a range of 30 feet. These effects last as long as you and the ally have their half; if either half leaves either of your possession for even a moment, or if you establish a link with a new ally during your next daily preparations, the link breaks.

<title level="2">Paired Link leads to...</title>
[Seven-Part Link](/Feats.aspx?ID=3728)
name: Paired Link

<trait label="Thaumaturge" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=432" />

</column>

---

Your studies into the supernatural have resulted in an especially strong knowledge of [rituals](/Rules.aspx?ID=777). You gain a +2 circumstance bonus to all primary checks to perform a ritual. You learn two [uncommon](/Traits.aspx?ID=159) [rituals](Rituals.aspx) with a level no higher than half your level; you must meet all prerequisites for casting the ritual to choose it. You can cast these as the primary caster, but you can't teach them to anyone else or allow someone else to serve as primary caster unless they know the ritual as well.

 At 8th level and every 4 levels thereafter, you learn another uncommon ritual with a level no higher than half your level and for which you meet the prerequisites.
name: Thaumaturgic Ritualist

You've forged a deeper bond to your invested items, allowing you to activate them more than usual. Once each day, you can Activate an Item you've invested even after you've used that activation the maximum number of times for its frequency. You can do so only if the item's level is half your level or lower, the activation has a frequency of once per day or more frequent, and you haven't already used the activation this round.

    right="Feat 6"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Scroll Esoterica](/Feats.aspx?ID=3713) <actions string="" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Esoterica" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=427" />
<trait label="Thaumaturge" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=432" />

</column>

---

Your esoterica includes scraps of scriptures, magic tomes, druidic markings, and the like, which you can use to create temporary [scrolls](/Equipment.aspx?Category=15&Subcategory=52). Each day during your daily preparations, you can create a single temporary scroll containing a 1st-level spell of any tradition. The spell must be common, or you must otherwise have access to it. This scroll is an unstable, temporary item and loses its magic the next time you make your daily preparations if you haven't used it by then. It can't be used to Learn the Spell.

 At 8th level, add a second temporary scroll containing a 2nd-level spell.

<title level="2">Scroll Esoterica leads to...</title>
[Elaborate Scroll Esoterica](/Feats.aspx?ID=3720)
name: Scroll Esoterica

<title
    level="1"
    right="Feat 6"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Sympathetic Vulnerabilities](/Feats.aspx?ID=3714) <actions string="" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Thaumaturge" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=432" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Dark Archive](/Sources.aspx?ID=129) pg. 45

**Prerequisites**
[Exploit Vulnerability](/Actions.aspx?ID=1227), mortal weakness or personal antithesis

</column>

---

When you apply your will to invoke a vulnerability, the result is more powerful, and the vulnerability ripples out in a web from your main target to affect a broader range of creatures. This has one of two effects, based on the type of vulnerability you invoked.

 While you have mortal weakness applied, your Strikes also apply that weakness against any creature that has that weakness, not just creatures of the exact same kind. For instance, if you used mortal weakness against a [red dragon](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=168) to apply its weakness to cold to your Strikes, those Strikes would also apply to the weakness to cold of [fire elementals](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=43) or any other creature with a weakness to cold.

 While you have personal antithesis applied to a non-[humanoid](/Traits.aspx?ID=91) creature, you can apply your custom weakness to all creatures of the exact same kind. For example, if you used personal antithesis against an [imp](/Monsters.aspx?ID=109), the custom weakness would apply to other imps but not to other non-imp [devils](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=30).
name: Sympathetic Vulnerabilities
prerequisite_markdown: [Exploit Vulnerability](/Actions.aspx?ID=1227), mortal weakness or personal antithesis

---

After your attack, you grab a bit of blood, cut hair, or other piece of the creature's body. You incorporate the material into a premade doll, paper figure, or other effigy to create a sympathetic link that makes it harder to resist your abilities. As long as you are Exploiting Vulnerability against that creature, it takes a –2 status penalty to its saving throws against thaumaturge abilities or items that use your thaumaturge class DC.
name: Cursed Effigy

Magical equipment and gear are the tools of your trade, and you know you need as many as possible to help you against the supernatural. You gain the [Incredible Investiture](/Feats.aspx?ID=795) skill feat, increasing your limit on invested items from 10 to 12. The limit increases to 14 if you have Charisma 18, 16 if you have Charisma 20, 18 if you have Charisma 22, and 20 if you have Charisma 24.

    right="Feat 10"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Twin Weakness](/Feats.aspx?ID=3719) <actions string="Two Actions" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Esoterica" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=427" />
<trait label="Thaumaturge" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=432" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Dark Archive](/Sources.aspx?ID=129) pg. 45

**Prerequisites**
mortal weakness or personal antithesis

**Requirements**
You're holding an [implement](Implements.aspx) and a weapon, you are using [Exploit Vulnerability](/Actions.aspx?ID=1227) with mortal weakness or personal antithesis, and the target creature is within reach of both your implement and weapon.

</column>

---

As you make an attack augmented by your esoterica, you also press your implement against the creature, applying its weakness as your implement's energies sear the creature's flesh. Make a melee Strike against the target of your Exploit Vulnerability. On any attack roll result but a critical failure, you also press your implement against the creature, automatically dealing the additional damage from Exploit Vulnerability. This is in addition to any damage from your Strike, including the weakness the Strike applies from Exploit Vulnerability. This counts as two attacks when calculating your multiple attack penalty.
name: Twin Weakness
requirement_markdown: You're holding an [implement](Implements.aspx) and a weapon, you are using [Exploit Vulnerability](/Actions.aspx?ID=1227) with mortal weakness or personal antithesis, and the target creature is within reach of both your implement and weapon.

mortal weakness or personal antithesis

**Requirements**
You're holding an [implement](Implements.aspx) and a weapon, you are using [Exploit Vulnerability](/Actions.aspx?ID=1227) with mortal weakness or personal antithesis, and the target creature is within reach of both your implement and weapon.

</additional-info>

---

<title
    level="1"
    right="Feat 12"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Thaumaturge's Demesne](/Feats.aspx?ID=3723) <actions string="" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Abjuration" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=2" />
<trait label="Arcane" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=11" />
<trait label="Thaumaturge" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=432" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Dark Archive](/Sources.aspx?ID=129) pg. 46

</column>

---

You have claimed an area or location as your demesne, granting you the ability to ward and protect it. When you select this feat, choose a demesne, an area of no more than 2,000 square feet. You must legitimately own the area or, if its a public area, you must lay claim to the area without anyone contesting it.

 Once you've chosen your demesne, it's automatically protected by an arcane [_private sanctum_](/Spells.aspx?ID=235) spell with an unlimited duration, heightened to half your level rounded up and using your thaumaturge class DC in place of a spell DC, if necessary. Additionally, the demesne is attended by three unseen custodians, as a successful [_unseen custodians_](/Rituals.aspx?ID=39) ritual, and one object in the area gains an elemental sentinel, as a successful [_elemental sentinel_](/Rituals.aspx?ID=57) ritual.

<title level="2">Thaumaturge's Demesne leads to...</title>
[Unlimited Demesne](/Feats.aspx?ID=3734)
name: Thaumaturge's Demesne

</title>

<traits>

<trait label="Occult" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=120" />

You are benefiting from Exploit Vulnerability.

</column>

---

You can hunt your foe to the ends of creation. You teleport along with the enemy, appearing the same direction and distance from it as you were before it teleported (or the nearest unoccupied space if your destination is occupied). Any allies affected by [Share Weakness](/Feats.aspx?ID=3717) or [Ubiquitous Weakness](/Feats.aspx?ID=3733), if you have those abilities, can spend their reaction to teleport along with the enemy if they're within 120 of the enemy and choose to do so.

Many traditions hold the number seven as significant. By exchanging pieces of a seven-part set of esoterica, you create a magical web by which your allies can affect each other at a distance. When you use Paired Link during your daily preparations, you can exchange linking esoterica with up to six willing allies, keeping one piece for yourself. In addition to the normal effects of Paired Link, if a linked ally casts a spell with a range of touch, they can target linked allies within 30 feet with that spell.

When you use [Exploit Vulnerability](/Actions.aspx?ID=1227) and choose mortal weakness, select any number of allies within 30 feet of you. You can move your demesne here and there, bringing it wherever your journey takes you. Your demesne expands to a maximum of 10,000 square feet. Once per day, you can call your demesne forth, which takes 1 minute. This has the effects of [_resplendent mansion_](/Spells.aspx?ID=257), except that it conjures your demesne from its previous location, with all the benefits of Thaumaturge's Demesne in addition to the those of the spell. You must be able to claim the new area, with the same restrictions as Thaumaturge's Demesne.
name: Unlimited Demesne

<title
    level="1"
    right="Feat 20"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Wonder Worker](/Feats.aspx?ID=3735) <actions string="" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Thaumaturge" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=432" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Dark Archive](/Sources.aspx?ID=129) pg. 47

**Prerequisites**
Legendary in [Arcana](/Skills.aspx?ID=2), Legendary in [Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=10), Legendary in [Occultism](/Skills.aspx?ID=11), or Legendary in [Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=13)

</column>

---

The thaumaturge's path culminates with the working of wonders. Once per day, you can align your esoterica to cast a spell of 8th level or lower that takes 1, 2, or 3 actions to cast. The spell must be common or one to which you have access. You can choose a spell of any tradition for which you're legendary in the associated skill (Arcana for [arcane](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=1), Nature for [primal](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=4), Occultism for [occult](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=3), or Religion for [divine](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=2)). Use your thaumaturge class DC in place of any necessary spell DC and your thaumaturge class DC – 10 in place of any necessary [counteract](/Rules.aspx?ID=371) modifier or spell attack modifier.
name: Wonder Worker
prerequisite_markdown: Legendary in [Arcana](/Skills.aspx?ID=2), Legendary in [Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=10), Legendary in [Occultism](/Skills.aspx?ID=11), or Legendary in [Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=13)

markdown:
<title
    level="1"
    right="Feat 2"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Psychic Dedication](/Feats.aspx?ID=3736) <actions string="" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Archetype" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=12" />
<trait label="Dedication" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=41" />
<trait label="Multiclass" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=115" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Dark Archive](/Sources.aspx?ID=129) pg. 48

**Archetype** [Psychic](/Archetypes.aspx?ID=185)

**Prerequisites**
Intelligence 14 or Charisma 14

</column>

---

You feel something awaken within your mind. You become trained in [Occultism](/Skills.aspx?ID=11); if you were already trained in Occultism, you become trained in a skill of your choice. You cast spells like a [psychic](/Classes.aspx?ID=21) and gain the Cast a Spell activity; as you don't have a subconscious mind, your thought components are simple intentions. Choose a [conscious mind](ConsciousMinds.aspx). You gain a spell repertoire with one standard psi cantrip of your choice from your conscious mind, which you cast as a psi cantrip. You gain the normal benefits and the amp for this psi cantrip, but not any other benefits from the conscious mind. If you don't have one, you gain a focus pool of 1 Focus Point, which you can use to amp your psi cantrips, and you can [Refocus](/Actions.aspx?ID=71) by meditating on your new powers. If you already have a focus pool, increase the number of points in your pool by 1. You're trained in occult spell attack rolls and occult spell DCs. Your key spellcasting ability for psychic archetype spells is the ability you used to qualify for the archetype, and they are occult psychic spells.

**Special** You can't select another dedication feat until you have gained two other feats from the psychic archetype.

<title level="2">Psychic Dedication leads to...</title>
[Basic Psychic Spellcasting](/Feats.aspx?ID=3738), [Basic Thoughtform](/Feats.aspx?ID=3737), [Psi Development](/Feats.aspx?ID=3740)
name: Psychic Dedication

<title
    level="1"
    right="Feat 2"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Thaumaturge Dedication](/Feats.aspx?ID=3743) <actions string="" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Archetype" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=12" />
<trait label="Dedication" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=41" />
<trait label="Multiclass" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=115" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Dark Archive](/Sources.aspx?ID=129) pg. 49

**Archetype** [Thaumaturge](/Archetypes.aspx?ID=186)

**Prerequisites**
Charisma 14

</column>

---

You've uncovered basic thaumaturgy. You become trained in thaumaturge class DC. Choose an [implement](Implements.aspx); you can use it to Glimpse Vulnerability, but don't gain its benefits. You also gain a few esoterica, allowing you to use [esoterica](/Traits.aspx?ID=427) actions. You become trained in your choice of [Arcana](/Skills.aspx?ID=2), [Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=10), [Occultism](/Skills.aspx?ID=11), or [Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=13); if you were already trained in these, you become trained in a skill of your choice. You gain the Glimpse Vulnerability action.

 **Glimpse Vulnerability** <actions string="Single Action" /> ([esoterica](/Traits.aspx?ID=427), [manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=104)); **Frequency** once per round; **Requirement** You are holding your implement; **Effect** You glimpse a hidden vulnerability and then strengthen it to aid you. Select a creature you can see. Until you Glimpse Vulnerability again, that target gains weakness 2 against your [unarmed](/Traits.aspx?ID=199) and weapon Strikes.

<title level="2">Thaumaturge Dedication leads to...</title>
[Basic Thaumaturgy](/Feats.aspx?ID=3744), [Implement Initiate](/Feats.aspx?ID=3746), [Magical Knowledge](/Feats.aspx?ID=3747), [Resolute](/Feats.aspx?ID=3748)
name: Thaumaturge Dedication

markdown:
<title
    level="1"
    right="Feat 4"
    pfs="Limited"
>
[Devil's Eye](/Feats.aspx?ID=3749) <actions string="" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Rare" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=137" />
<trait label="Aftermath" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=424" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Dark Archive](/Sources.aspx?ID=129) pg. 55

**Prerequisites**
You made a bargain with a [devil](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=30) or won a legal case or other similar duel of wits against a devil.

</column>

---

Your experience in the finer points of bargaining with a devil has awakened a sinister power that now inhabits your left eye. The eye is the same shape and iris color as your normal eye would be, but the sclera is a deep red instead of white and the pupil is a narrow, vertical slit. The eye grants you an abnormal understanding of the loopholes and legal processes employed to trap poor creatures that don't know any better. You gain the Objection reaction. You also become an expert in [Legal Lore](/Skills.aspx?ID=8) and gain a +1 circumstance bonus to any check you make to read or negotiate a treatise, contract, or similar text. At 7th level you become a master of Legal Lore, and at 15th level you become legendary in Legal Lore. You can use Legal Lore in place of Perception to detect verbal loopholes, double entendres, hidden meanings, code, or contradictions in a conversation you can hear. This doesn't let you fully understand coded speech but alerts you to its presence.

 **Objection** <actions string="Reaction" /> ([fortune](/Traits.aspx?ID=76)) **Frequency** once per minute; **Trigger** You're about to attempt a saving throw against a [linguistic](/Traits.aspx?ID=101) effect; **Effect** Your devil's eye crackles with infernal glee as you discover a loophole in the wording of the triggering effect. You roll your saving throw twice, taking the higher result.
name: Devil's Eye
prerequisite_markdown: You made a bargain with a [devil](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=30) or won a legal case or other similar duel of wits against a devil.

markdown:
<title
    level="1"
    right="Feat 4"
    pfs="Limited"
>
[Echo of the Fallen](/Feats.aspx?ID=3750) <actions string="" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Rare" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=137" />
<trait label="Aftermath" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=424" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Dark Archive](/Sources.aspx?ID=129) pg. 55

**Prerequisites**
You helped to lay a [haunt](/Traits.aspx?ID=88), [ghost](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=51), or [spirit](/Traits.aspx?ID=149) to rest.

</column>

---

With the utmost gratitude for your help in releasing them from their unearthly coil, a tiny sliver of a ghostly entity has remained attached to you. This manifests as an echo of their former self that follows you around and that you and anyone capable of casting [divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=48) or [occult](/Traits.aspx?ID=120) spells can see and speak with. When you take this feat, work with your GM to choose one martial weapon and one skill (or an advanced weapon if you're trained in all martial weapons) that are appropriate for the spirit; once you've chosen the weapon and skill, you can't change them. You gain the Accept Echo action.

 **Accept Echo** <actions string="Single Action" /> ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=32)) **Frequency** once per day; **Effect** You allow the echo to inhabit your body, gaining a glowing aura around you, shining pale-green irises, a resonating voice, and the silhouette of the spirit around your own body, all of which can be seen clearly by others. While in this state, you become trained in the weapon you chose and an expert in the skill you chose. Additionally, while you're in this state, the echo can speak and interact with the [Material Plane](/Planes.aspx?ID=1) using your body, and you gain a +4 status bonus to all saves against possession effects, as the possessing spirit protects you from other possessions.

 This state lasts for 10 minutes, after which the echo disappears completely until the next time you make your daily preparations. You can Dismiss this effect.

 At 7th level you become a master of the skill you chose, at 13th level you become an expert with the weapon you chose, and at 15th level you become legendary with the skill you chose.
name: Echo of the Fallen
prerequisite_markdown: You helped to lay a [haunt](/Traits.aspx?ID=88), [ghost](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=51), or [spirit](/Traits.aspx?ID=149) to rest.
search_markdown:
<traits>
<trait label="Rare" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=137" />
<trait label="Aftermath" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=424" />
</traits>

<additional-info>
**Source** [Dark Archive](/Sources.aspx?ID=129) pg. 55

**Prerequisites**

    right="Feat 4"
    pfs="Limited"
>
[Lingering Chill](/Feats.aspx?ID=3751) <actions string="" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Rare" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=137" />
<trait label="Aftermath" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=424" />
<trait label="Cold" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=27" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Dark Archive](/Sources.aspx?ID=129) pg. 55

**Prerequisites**
You've been brought to 0 Hit Points by an enemy that has the [cold](/Traits.aspx?ID=27) trait or an enemy's ability that has the cold trait.

</column>

---

The chill of ice entered your veins and never left, adjusting the internal temperature of your body. Your breath comes out in puffs, and your skin is as cold and smooth as the ice in your veins. You can cast [_ray of frost_](/Spells.aspx?ID=245) as a primal innate cantrip. You gain resistance to cold damage equal to half your level, and you're protected from [severe cold temperatures](/Rules.aspx?ID=642).

You gain a +1 circumstance bonus to checks to [Make an Impression](/Actions.aspx?ID=50), or a +2 circumstance bonus if the target is chaotic good. You gain the Set Free reaction.

 **Set Free** <actions string="Reaction" /> ([fortune](/Traits.aspx?ID=76)) **Frequency** once per hour; **Trigger** You attempt a check to remove or [counteract](/Rules.aspx?ID=371) an effect with the [confused](/Conditions.aspx?ID=5), [controlled](/Conditions.aspx?ID=6), [fascinated](/Conditions.aspx?ID=14), [immobilized](/Conditions.aspx?ID=24), [paralyzed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=28), or [restrained](/Conditions.aspx?ID=33) conditions; **Effect** You roll twice and use the better result.

---

Some of the ooze that surrounded you made its way permanently into parts of your body, turning you soft to the touch and semitransparent enough to show bones. You gain resistance equal to your level to precision damage and damage from critical hits. You can submerge up to 1 Bulk of items within your body by sticking them inside of yourself. While the protrusion of the item is still visible from the outside, the items don't count toward making you encumbered or your maximum Bulk while stored in you. You can Interact to retrieve or store an item submerged into your body.

<traits>
<trait label="Rare" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=137" />
<trait label="Aftermath" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=424" />

**Prerequisites**
You have succeeded at an important task given by a [dragon](/Traits.aspx?ID=50), such as procuring a special treasure for their hoard.

</column>

---

In pleasing the cravings of a mighty dragon, they have deigned to impart some of their magical essence into your body. Your eyes begin to glow and change color while your limbs and neck grow a veneer of scales, all the same color as the associated dragon, accompanied by a near-insatiable desire to amass a hoard of your own. You gain the [_dragon breath_](/Spells.aspx?ID=499) sorcerer bloodline spell, emulating the breath of the associated dragon, and a focus pool of 1 Focus Point. If you already have a focus pool, increase your number of Focus Points by 1. You [Refocus](/Actions.aspx?ID=71) by counting and arranging your treasure.
name: Gift of the Hoard

**Success** The enemy takes half the additional negative damage.
**Failure** The enemy takes full additional negative damage.
**Critical Failure** The enemy takes double the additional negative damage.

<title
    level="1"
    right="Feat 12"
    pfs="Limited"
>
[Petrified Skin](/Feats.aspx?ID=3756) <actions string="" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Rare" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=137" />
<trait label="Aftermath" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=424" />
<trait label="Transmutation" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=157" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Dark Archive](/Sources.aspx?ID=129) pg. 56

**Prerequisites**
You have been [petrified](/Conditions.aspx?ID=30) by an enemy.

</column>

---

It might have been a [medusa](/Monsters.aspx?ID=297), [dracolisk](/Monsters.aspx?ID=565), or even a [fossil golem](/Monsters.aspx?ID=1174); regardless of the source, you were the target of some petrifying effect, and an element of that stony gaze has remained with you, both protecting and slowly consuming you. Your limbs are coated with a layer of stone that rests atop your skin and covers more of your body as you lose health. You gain a stone fist [unarmed](/Traits.aspx?ID=199) attack that deals 1d8 bludgeoning damage, has the [shove](/Actions.aspx?ID=38) trait, and is in the [brawling](/WeaponGroups.aspx?ID=4) weapon group (unlike a normal fist, it doesn't have the [agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=170) or [finesse](/Traits.aspx?ID=179) traits).

 Additionally, you become more petrified as your life force ebbs. When you have fewer than half your maximum Hit Points, you increase the damage die size of your stone fist from 1d8 to 1d10 and gain resistance to physical damage (except [adamantine](/Equipment.aspx?ID=374)) equal to your Constitution modifier.

 If you would gain the [dying](/Conditions.aspx?ID=11) condition, you can choose to instead be permanently petrified and avoid the risk of death. When you do, you enter your petrified form with your full maximum Hit Points instead of the normal number. If you become unpetrified, you return to 0 Hit Points, as normal. You can't voluntarily end this petrification. [Counteracting](/Rules.aspx?ID=371) this petrification requires a casting of [_stone to flesh_](/Spells.aspx?ID=311) of a spell level equal to at least half your level, and a successful counteract check against the hard DC for your level. Other effects like _stone to flesh_ have similar limitations, as determined by the GM. Each time you recover from this petrification, you gain a new scar on your skin in the shape of a long, thin crack.
name: Petrified Skin
prerequisite_markdown: You have been [petrified](/Conditions.aspx?ID=30) by an enemy.

---

A sweltering heat and sparks of flame that take the form of tiny copies of you surround your body. You're hot to the touch, gaining resistance 5 to cold and fire. Whenever an adjacent creature Strikes you with a melee attack or touches you, it takes 1d6 [persistent fire damage](/Conditions.aspx?ID=29). You gain the Thermal Eruption action.

 **Thermal Eruption** <actions string="Two Actions" /> ([evocation](/Traits.aspx?ID=65), [fire](/Traits.aspx?ID=72), [primal](/Traits.aspx?ID=134)) **Frequency** once per day; **Effect** You concentrate your thermal energy and explode it outward. All creatures in a 20-foot emanation take 14d6 fire damage with a [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=329) Reflex save. Afterward, you lose all effects of the Dormant Eruption feat until your next daily preparations.

---

Your body looks like it's made of flowing water, clear and pristine, providing a natural grace to all your movements. You gain a swim Speed equal to your land Speed. If you already had a swim Speed, it's increased by 10 feet. You gain the Water Transfer activity, which enables you to teleport through a body of water.

 **Water Transfer** <actions string="Single Action" /> ([conjuration](/Traits.aspx?ID=33), [primal](/Traits.aspx?ID=134), [teleportation](/Traits.aspx?ID=156), [water](/Traits.aspx?ID=165)) **Frequency** once per minute; **Requirements** You're on land and adjacent to a body of water; **Effect** You sink into the water and emerge back onto land in another space within 120 feet that's adjacent to the same body of water. You can transport yourself, any items you're wearing and holding, and up to one other willing creature.

markdown:
<title
    level="1"
    right="Feat 16"
    pfs="Limited"
>
[Fey Life](/Feats.aspx?ID=3759) <actions string="" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Rare" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=137" />
<trait label="Aftermath" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=424" />
<trait label="Healing" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=89" />
<trait label="Necromancy" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=117" />
<trait label="Primal" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=134" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Dark Archive](/Sources.aspx?ID=129) pg. 57

**Prerequisites**
You helped to save a [fey](/Traits.aspx?ID=69) from a terrible fate, and you're not a fey.

</column>

---

There are some fates worse than death–and you worked to save a fey from one such fate. In return, your life became bound to theirs. Fey magic has since become a part of you, bringing the vibrant colors of the [First World](/Planes.aspx?ID=10) to you and all that surrounds you. Your skin, hair, all that you touch, and anything within 5 feet of you changes to the most brilliant hues possible, though the colors fade as you cease to touch an object or you move away. You gain the ability to cast [_summon fey_](/Spells.aspx?ID=322) as a 7th-level primal innate spell once per day. At 18th level the spell heightens to 8th level, and at 20th level it heightens to 9th level.

 The first time you die after gaining this feat, the bond you made with the fey invokes the power of the First World to return you to life. Immediately after dying, you revive, becoming conscious (and [wounded](/Conditions.aspx?ID=42) as normal) at 27 Hit Points, and you gain the fey trait. Your eyes become a rainbow of colors, and you take on a visual aspect of the fey with whom you are bound, such as by growing wings, horns, or a plantlike body. The change has no mechanical effect; for instance, the wings wouldn't allow you to fly, nor would horns grant a horn unarmed attack.

 The second time you die, you reawaken in the First World with the full appearance of the fey that was bound to you. You become an NPC, unconcerned with your old mortal life. You can be returned to your previous life in the [Material Plane](/Planes.aspx?ID=1) by having allies spend 3 days building a shrine to the First World, giving three offerings that hold deep sentimental value for you or another, and holding a raucous, three-day long feast.

 Any time that you die after that, you reawaken again in the First World with no memories of your previous life, and you can only be brought back by powerful magic such as a [_wish_](/Spells.aspx?ID=377) spell.
name: Fey Life
prerequisite_markdown: You helped to save a [fey](/Traits.aspx?ID=69) from a terrible fate, and you're not a fey.

You're light on your feet, your voice gains an airy quality that allows it to travel further than normal, and your clothing and hair rustle gently around you at all times. You can Stride at half your land Speed across calm air. If you attempt to carry another creature with you while walking on air, even if it's in an [extradimensional](/Traits.aspx?ID=67) space, you fall.

<trait label="Metamagic" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=107" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Dark Archive](/Sources.aspx?ID=129) pg. 64

**Requirements**
You're in natural terrain.

</column>

---

You hide your magic in the croak of a frog, in the sway of the trees, in the howl of the wind, and the flicker of the will-o'-wisp. If the next action you take is to Cast a Spell, attempt a [Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=10) check against all observers' Perception DCs. If you succeed at your check against an observer's Perception DC, that observer doesn't notice that you're Casting a Spell, even though verbal, somatic, and material components are usually noticeable, and spells normally have sensory manifestations that would make spellcasting obvious to those nearby. All of these are instead hidden in the natural sights and sounds of the environment.

 This ability hides only the spell's spellcasting actions and manifestations, not its effects, so an observer might still see you transform into a giant bear.
name: Cryptic Spell
requirement_markdown: You're in natural terrain.

<title
    level="1"
    right="Feat 4"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Wolf in Sheep's Clothing](/Feats.aspx?ID=3762) <actions string="" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Ranger" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=136" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Dark Archive](/Sources.aspx?ID=129) pg. 64

**Prerequisites**
Trained in [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=5)

</column>

---

With a bit of hide, some antlers, and some cast-off fur, you can create a disguise that will fool even an experienced hunter. You can [Impersonate](/Actions.aspx?ID=46) creatures with the [animal](/Traits.aspx?ID=9), [beast](/Traits.aspx?ID=20), or [plant](/Traits.aspx?ID=125) traits, so long as they are either the same size or one size larger than you and have a body shape that conforms at least vaguely to your own (in other words, a typical humanoid could Impersonate a [bear](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=13) or an [arboreal](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=8), but not a [horse](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=62), [giant scorpion](/Monsters.aspx?ID=356), or [assassin vine](/Monsters.aspx?ID=555)). Impersonating a larger creature doesn't change your actual size.
name: Wolf in Sheep's Clothing

markdown:
<title
    level="1"
    right="Feat 8"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Can't You See?](/Feats.aspx?ID=3763) <actions string="" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Druid" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=52" />
<trait label="Ranger" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=136" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Dark Archive](/Sources.aspx?ID=129) pg. 64

**Prerequisites**
Trained in [Occultism](/Skills.aspx?ID=11); Expert in [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=15)

</column>

---

The eye rebels, the mind recoils—no matter how much those who can see you try to explain what's there, their friends' gazes just skitter over you, like a bird afraid to land. A character who attempts to [Point Out](/Actions.aspx?ID=97) your location must attempt a DC 14 flat check. If they fail, their allies misunderstand them and aren't sure where you are. On a critical failure, their allies think they pointed you out in a different location entirely, chosen by the GM. Similarly, when a creature critically fails to [Seek](/Actions.aspx?ID=84) you while you're [hidden](/Conditions.aspx?ID=22) to or [undetected](/Conditions.aspx?ID=39) by it, it thinks you're in a different location chosen by the GM. In either case, you appear to be hidden to a creature that thinks you're in a different location, though you're actually undetected by it for targeting and further uses of the Seek action.
name: Can't You See?

<title
    level="1"
    right="Feat 8"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Eerie Environs](/Feats.aspx?ID=3764) <actions string="" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Druid" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=52" />
<trait label="Ranger" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=136" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Dark Archive](/Sources.aspx?ID=129) pg. 65

**Prerequisites**
Trained in [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=7); Trained in [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=15)

</column>

---

The natural world can be scary to those not used to it—and you make it scarier still. If you're hidden from a creature, you can attempt to [Demoralize](/Actions.aspx?ID=53) it without losing your [hidden](/Conditions.aspx?ID=22) condition— imitating the sounds of strange beasts or causing the foliage to rustle menacingly. When you do so, you don't take a penalty to your check if the target doesn't understand your language.
name: Eerie Environs
